User.title: User
User.description: Represents an individual user registered with the application. This entity stores user profile information, with authentication handled by an external system like Firebase Authentication.

id.description: Unique identifier for the User entity. This will typically be the user ID provided by the authentication system (e.g., Firebase UID).

email.description: The email address of the user, used for login and identification.

displayName.description: An optional display name chosen by the user for personalization.

createdAt.description: Timestamp indicating when the user account was created.

lastLoginAt.description: Timestamp indicating the user's most recent login.

SatelliteImage.title: SatelliteImage
SatelliteImage.description: Represents a satellite image uploaded by a user for processing and analysis.

id.description: Unique identifier for the SatelliteImage entity.

fileName.description: The original file name of the uploaded image.

storagePath.description: The URL or path where the image file is stored (e.g., Firebase Storage URL).

uploadDateTime.description: Timestamp indicating when the image was uploaded.

originalWidth.description: The original width of the image in pixels, useful for CRS.Simple mapping.

originalHeight.description: The original height of the image in pixels, useful for CRS.Simple mapping.

DetectionSession.title: DetectionSession
DetectionSession.description: Represents a single attempt or session of performing AI-powered building detection on a specific satellite image.

id.description: Unique identifier for the DetectionSession entity.

sessionStartDateTime.description: Timestamp indicating when the detection session was initiated.

sessionEndDateTime.description: Timestamp indicating when the detection session completed or failed. Can be null if still pending.

status.description: The current status of the detection session (e.g., 'pending', 'processing', 'completed', 'failed').

processingOutputGeoJson.description: The GeoJSON string containing the AI-detected building polygons. Stored as a string to allow for flexible GeoJSON structures.

ClickedCoordinate.title: ClickedCoordinate
ClickedCoordinate.description: Represents a specific [x, y] coordinate clicked by the user on a satellite image, used as input for building detection within a session.

id.description: Unique identifier for the ClickedCoordinate entity.

xCoordinate.description: The X-coordinate (pixel column) clicked on the image.

yCoordinate.description: The Y-coordinate (pixel row) clicked on the image.

clickDateTime.description: Timestamp indicating when the coordinate was clicked.

definition.description: Stores individual user profile information. Each document is uniquely identified by the Firebase Authentication UID, which matches the {userId} wildcard. This collection is private to the owning user.

params.name: userId
params.description: The unique identifier of the user, corresponding to their Firebase Authentication UID.

definition.description: Stores metadata for satellite images uploaded by a specific user. Access is restricted to the owner specified by the {userId} in the path. Includes denormalized 'userId' field for authorization independence.

params.name: userId
params.description: The unique identifier of the user who owns this image.

params.name: imageId
params.description: The unique identifier for the satellite image.

definition.description: Represents AI-powered building detection sessions performed on a specific satellite image by its owner. Access is restricted to the owner defined by {userId} in the path. Includes denormalized 'userId' and 'imageId' fields for authorization independence.

params.name: userId
params.description: The unique identifier of the user who initiated this session.

params.name: imageId
params.description: The unique identifier of the satellite image on which this session was run.

params.name: sessionId
params.description: The unique identifier for this detection session.

definition.description: Stores individual [x, y] coordinates clicked by the user within a specific detection session. Access is restricted to the owner defined by {userId} in the path. Includes denormalized 'detectionSessionId' field for authorization independence.

params.name: userId
params.description: The unique identifier of the user who owns the parent detection session.

params.name: imageId
params.description: The unique identifier of the satellite image associated with the parent detection session.

params.name: sessionId
params.description: The unique identifier of the detection session to which this coordinate belongs.

params.name: coordinateId
params.description: The unique identifier for this clicked coordinate.

firestore.reasoning:
The recommended Firestore structure is designed to adhere strictly to the core design principles, especially Authorization Independence and QAPs, by leveraging a deeply hierarchical, user-owned data model. Each user's data is siloed under their unique `userId` within the `/users` top-level collection. This creates a highly secure and easily auditable data access pattern.

1.  **Authorization Independence:** This is achieved primarily through path-based ownership and inherent denormalization. For instance, a `SatelliteImage` is stored at `/users/{userId}/satelliteImages/{imageId}`. Any Firestore security rule for this collection can simply compare `request.auth.uid` directly against the `{userId}` wildcard in the path. This eliminates the need for expensive and complex `get()` calls to parent documents (e.g., to check if the parent `User` document belongs to `request.auth.uid`), thereby preventing rule complexity and avoiding breaking atomic operations.
    
    *   `User` documents (`/users/{userId}`) are directly owned by `request.auth.uid`.
    *   `SatelliteImage` documents (`/users/{userId}/satelliteImages/{imageId}`) implicitly carry their `userId` in the path. While the `SatelliteImage` entity *also* contains a `userId` field, its primary role in authorization comes from the path, making it self-contained.
    *   `DetectionSession` documents (`/users/{userId}/satelliteImages/{imageId}/detectionSessions/{sessionId}`) similarly have `userId` and `imageId` in their path, making their ownership and context immediately clear without parent lookups.
    *   `ClickedCoordinate` documents (`/users/{userId}/satelliteImages/{imageId}/detectionSessions/{sessionId}/clickedCoordinates/{coordinateId}`) continue this pattern, ensuring that all necessary authorization context is available directly from the path.

2.  **QAPs (Queries as Authorization Primitives):** By segregating data under `/users/{userId}/...`, `list` operations become inherently secure and performant. A user can only query for data within their own `/users/{request.auth.uid}/...` subtrees. For example, a query to fetch all satellite images for the current user would target `/users/{request.auth.uid}/satelliteImages`. The security rules can then simply enforce `allow list: if request.auth.uid == userId;`, ensuring that the database only returns documents explicitly owned by the requesting user, with no client-side filtering required. This prevents data leakage and simplifies client-side logic.

3.  **Structural Segregation:** Each collection (`users`, `satelliteImages`, `detectionSessions`, `clickedCoordinates`) maintains a homogeneous security posture (private, user-owned data), simplifying security rules further. There are no mixed-access data types within the same collection.

4.  **Data Clarity and Predictability:** The use of descriptive wildcards (`{userId}`, `{imageId}`, `{sessionId}`, `{coordinateId}`) and explicit entity names enhances readability and debuggability. The `status` field in `DetectionSession` exemplifies explicit state modeling.
